commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: A narrow security repair is available: extend this PR to use a mandatory bootstrap-token limiter rather than the optional configured auth limiter.; Needs attention: The diff improves the attack path but leaves the default bootstrap-token mutex DoS path unthrottled for unconfigured and loopback-exempt clients.; Review finding: [P1] Use an always-on limiter for bootstrap token checks — src/gateway/server/ws-connection... (sha=708e39903852650a3e527973637e926ff7e864af)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a bootstrap-token auth rate-limit scope, gates bootstrap-token verification, adds unit and WebSocket integration tests, and records a changelog entry.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection gives a high-confidence path: current main accepts a signed device identity plus auth.bootstrapToken, then enters verifyDeviceBootstrapToken before any bootstrap-token limiter; the PR also describes an integration test that exercises this path.
commands.review_followup: A narrow security repair is available: extend this PR to use a mandatory bootstrap-token limiter rather than the optional configured auth limiter.

target.title: fix(gateway): rate-limit pre-auth bootstrap-token verify to prevent mutex DoS
target.author: fede-kamel
